hero.headline: Kurukshetra University Online MBA in Human Resource Management: Syllabus, Fees and Career Outcomes
hero.sub: UGC-DEB approved 2-year programme | NAAC A++ accredited | Public state university | Rs 1,02,000 total fee

sections.tldr: Kurukshetra University Online MBA in Human Resource Management covers HRD Systems and Strategies, Indian Labour Legislation, Management of Industrial Relations, Human Resource Metrics and Analytics, Compensation and Reward Management, Talent Acquisition and Performance Management, Strategic Human Resource Management, Global Human Resource Management, HR Analytics, and Counseling Skills for Managers. Total fee Rs 1,02,000 (Rs 25,500/semester). NAAC A++ accredited public state university. Zero-cost EMI available directly through the university.

about.body:
Kurukshetra University's Online MBA in Human Resource Management combines traditional HR (industrial relations, labour legislation, compensation management) with modern HR practice (HR analytics, talent acquisition, global HRM, counseling skills). At Rs 1,02,000 total, this is the only NAAC A++ public state university offering a structured online MBA in HR at this fee tier.

The curriculum stands out in two ways. First, Indian Labour Legislation and Management of Industrial Relations as standalone Sem 3 papers give this programme an IR/compliance depth that most private university online MBAs lack. For candidates in manufacturing, government, or unionized sectors, this is directly applicable. Second, Counseling Skills for Managers in Sem 4 is an unusual and practical subject for HR professionals handling employee grievances, performance conversations, and conflict resolution.

Kurukshetra University is a state university established in 1956 under the Haryana government. NAAC A++ (the highest possible grade), UGC-DEB approved for online programmes. As a public university, tuition is government-subsidized, which explains the lower fee compared to private online MBA providers.

EdifyEdu verifies data independently and takes no commission from any university.

syllabus.note: Semesters 1-2 cover the common MBA core. Semesters 3-4 below show the Human Resource specialisation subjects plus common core papers (Strategic Management, Optimization Models, Summer Training in Sem 3; Entrepreneurship Development, Business Ethics and Corporate Governance, Comprehensive Viva-Voce in Sem 4).

subjects.name: Strategic Management
subjects.desc: Common core. Competitive strategy frameworks, industry analysis, corporate-level strategy, and strategic planning process.

subjects.name: Optimization Models for Business Decisions
subjects.desc: Common core. Linear programming, transportation models, assignment problems, and quantitative decision-making tools.

subjects.name: Summer Training/Project Work
subjects.desc: Common core. Industry-based project applying management concepts to a real business problem.

subjects.name: HRD Systems and Strategies
subjects.desc: Human resource development frameworks, training needs assessment, career development systems, and organizational learning strategies.

subjects.name: Indian Labour Legislation
subjects.desc: Factories Act, Industrial Disputes Act, Minimum Wages Act, PF and ESI regulations, and the new Labour Codes framework.

subjects.name: Management of Industrial Relations
subjects.desc: Trade union dynamics, collective bargaining, dispute resolution mechanisms, tripartite consultation, and IR in Indian manufacturing.

subjects.name: Human Resource Metrics and Analytics
subjects.desc: HR dashboards, attrition analysis, cost-per-hire calculation, employee engagement metrics, and predictive workforce analytics.

subjects.name: Compensation and Reward Management
subjects.desc: Salary structure design, variable pay frameworks, benefits administration, compensation benchmarking, and total rewards strategy.

subjects.name: Talent Acquisition and Performance Management
subjects.desc: Recruitment strategy, employer branding, competency-based hiring, performance appraisal systems, and goal-setting frameworks (OKR, KPI).

subjects.name: Entrepreneurship Development
subjects.desc: Common core. Startup ideation, business plan creation, funding strategies, and entrepreneurial ecosystem in India.

subjects.name: Business Ethics and Corporate Governance
subjects.desc: Common core. Ethical frameworks, corporate social responsibility, board governance, and regulatory compliance.

subjects.name: Comprehensive Viva-Voce
subjects.desc: Common core. Oral examination covering all four semesters of the MBA programme.

subjects.name: Strategic Human Resource Management
subjects.desc: HR strategy alignment with business goals, workforce planning, HR business partnering, and strategic HR transformation.

subjects.name: Global Human Resource Management
subjects.desc: Expatriate management, cross-cultural HR practices, international compensation, global mobility, and multinational HR compliance.

subjects.name: HR Analytics
subjects.desc: Advanced people analytics, predictive turnover models, workforce optimization, sentiment analysis, and data-driven HR decision-making.

subjects.name: Counseling Skills for Managers
subjects.desc: Employee counseling techniques, active listening, conflict resolution, handling grievances, and supporting employee wellbeing.

whoHires.body:
Kurukshetra University MBA HR graduates target roles across corporate HR, industrial relations, and HR consulting:

**Industries:** IT/ITES, manufacturing, banking, FMCG, consulting, government HR departments, PSUs, healthcare, and logistics

**Entry-level roles:** HR Executive, Talent Acquisition Specialist, Payroll Executive, Employee Engagement Coordinator, HR Compliance Officer, Training Coordinator

**Mid-level roles (3-5 years experience):** HR Manager, HR Business Partner, Compensation and Benefits Manager, Learning and Development Manager, IR Manager, Talent Acquisition Lead

**Senior roles (7+ years):** CHRO (mid-market), VP HR, Head of Talent, Director of Employee Relations, Head of HR Operations, Head of L&D

**Employers:** IT companies (TCS, Infosys, Wipro), manufacturing firms, PSUs, banks, FMCG companies, consulting firms, and Haryana/NCR regional corporates.

**Note:** As a public state university with NAAC A++, the Kurukshetra MBA degree carries strong credibility for government HR, PSU HR departments, and IR roles in manufacturing. Indian Labour Legislation and Industrial Relations subjects add direct value for compliance-heavy sectors.

skills.body:
The Human Resource specialisation builds skills across talent management, analytics, IR/compliance, and strategic HR:

**Technical skills:**
- HR Analytics: dashboard creation, attrition prediction, cost-per-hire analysis, engagement survey interpretation, and workforce optimization models
- Compensation Management: salary structure design, CTC calculation, variable pay plan creation, benefits benchmarking, and total rewards modelling
- Labour Law Compliance: Factories Act application, PF/ESI calculation, Industrial Disputes Act procedures, and new Labour Codes implementation
- Talent Acquisition: competency framework design, structured interview scorecards, employer branding strategy, and ATS workflow management

**Applied skills:**
- Industrial Relations: collective bargaining preparation, dispute resolution documentation, union negotiation, and IR audit processes
- Global HRM: expatriate compensation design, cross-cultural training programme creation, and international mobility policy drafting

**Soft skills:** Employee counseling, active listening in grievance handling, conflict mediation, performance conversation delivery, and presenting HR strategy to business leadership.

comparisons.body:
Comparing Kurukshetra University Online MBA HR against peer programmes:

**vs Vignan Online MBA HRM (Rs 90,000):** Vignan is Rs 12,000 cheaper with NAAC A+ and NIRF #70. Vignan covers training and development, OB, and HR consulting. Kurukshetra at Rs 1,02,000 is a NAAC A++ public university with Indian Labour Legislation, Industrial Relations, HR Analytics, and Counseling Skills as standalone subjects. Kurukshetra wins on accreditation grade, public university status, and IR/compliance depth.

**vs Galgotias Online MBA HR (Rs 86,400):** Galgotias is Rs 15,600 cheaper with NAAC A+ and NCR location. Both cover talent management and compensation. Kurukshetra adds labour law, industrial relations, and counseling skills as separate papers with NAAC A++. Pay more at Kurukshetra for the highest NAAC grade and public university credibility.

**vs Uttaranchal Online MBA HR (Rs 94,000):** Uttaranchal at Rs 94,000 is Rs 8,000 cheaper with NAAC A. Both are affordable options. Kurukshetra has a significantly higher NAAC grade (A++ vs A) and adds Indian Labour Legislation and Industrial Relations depth. Kurukshetra wins on accreditation and IR/compliance curriculum.

**Key differentiator:** Kurukshetra is the only NAAC A++ public university in this fee tier. Indian Labour Legislation and Industrial Relations as standalone subjects add genuine value for manufacturing, government, and compliance-focused HR roles.

fees.body:
The Kurukshetra University Online MBA in Human Resource Management is priced at Rs 1,02,000 total program fee across 4 semesters (2-year programme). As a NAAC A++ accredited public state university, Kurukshetra offers government-backed education at significantly lower fees than most private online MBA programmes.

**Payment options**:
- **Semester-wise**: Rs 25,500 per semester x 4 installments
- **Full upfront**: Rs 1,02,000 one-time

**Additional charges**: Specific registration, application, examination, or project fees are not separately documented on the Kurukshetra Online portal. Please reconfirm all charges with our counsellor before enrolment.

**Zero-cost EMI**: Available directly through Kurukshetra University (no external marketing partner or NBFC intermediary). Contact our counsellor for current tenure options.

EdifyEdu reconfirms fees with Kurukshetra University each quarter. Please reconfirm current fees and all applicable charges with our counsellor before any payment. Fees listed are indicative.

certificate.body:
Graduates receive an MBA degree certificate with Human Resource Management as the specialisation, issued by Kurukshetra University under UGC-DEB online mode standards.

The degree carries NAAC A++ accreditation (the highest possible grade) and UGC-DEB approval. As a public state university degree, it holds strong validity for government recruitment, PSU HR roles, and private-sector HR management positions. DigiLocker issuance is standard.

reviews.intro: Three reviews from Kurukshetra University Online MBA HR students. Ratings reflect individual experience.

items.name: Kavita J.
items.liked: NAAC A++ public state university at Rs 1,02,000. For PSU and government HR roles, a state university MBA carries more weight than private alternatives
items.disliked: The online platform works but is not as feature-rich as Amity or LPU. Mobile app experience needs improvement
items.body: I work in a manufacturing firm where labour law compliance and industrial relations are daily realities. Kurukshetra's HR programme has Indian Labour Legislation and Management of Industrial Relations as full papers. That is exactly what I needed. The public university degree with NAAC A++ gives me credibility for internal promotions and government job applications. The fee at Rs 25,500 per semester is genuinely affordable.

items.name: Deepak S.
items.liked: Counseling Skills for Managers in Sem 4 is a unique subject. It directly helps with employee grievance handling and difficult performance conversations
items.disliked: Kurukshetra brand is strong in Haryana and parts of North India but recognition drops in South and West India. National MNC employers may not immediately know the university
items.body: The HR curriculum covers both traditional and modern HR well. HRD Systems, Compensation Management, Talent Acquisition, and HR Analytics give a solid foundation. The labour law and industrial relations papers add depth for compliance roles. Counseling Skills is genuinely useful. The brand limitation is regional. In Haryana, Punjab, and NCR the Kurukshetra name is well-regarded. Nationally, you may need to lead with the NAAC A++ public university positioning.

items.name: Ritu P.
items.liked: Zero-cost EMI directly through the university. No third-party NBFC involved. The semester-wise payment of Rs 25,500 is manageable
items.disliked: Some recorded lectures for HR Analytics could be more data-driven with practical tool demonstrations. Career services for online students are basic
items.body: The programme covers what it should. The syllabus is strong, especially the IR and labour law content. Delivery quality is mixed. Some faculty are excellent, others rely on slides. HR Analytics could use more hands-on tool training. At this price from a NAAC A++ public university, the value is clear. If you need premium delivery and active placement support, you will need to spend more at a private university.

faqs.question: Is Kurukshetra a public or private university?
faqs.answer: Kurukshetra University is a public state university established under the Haryana government. Public universities receive government funding, which keeps tuition lower than private institutions. This also means the degree carries state university recognition for government and PSU recruitment.

faqs.question: Why is the Kurukshetra Online MBA cheaper than Amity or NMIMS?
faqs.answer: Because Kurukshetra is a government-funded public state university. Private universities like Amity (Rs 3L+) and NMIMS (Rs 2.5L+) set their own fee structures. Public universities receive state subsidies that keep tuition significantly lower. Kurukshetra at Rs 1,02,000 total reflects this public university pricing model.

faqs.question: Does Kurukshetra offer zero-cost EMI?
faqs.answer: Yes. Zero-cost EMI is available directly through Kurukshetra University. No external NBFC or marketing partner is involved. Contact our counsellor for the current EMI tenure and monthly amount.

faqs.question: Does the HR MBA cover Indian labour laws and industrial relations?
faqs.answer: Yes. Indian Labour Legislation and Management of Industrial Relations are standalone Sem 3 subjects. They cover Factories Act, Industrial Disputes Act, PF/ESI, new Labour Codes, collective bargaining, and dispute resolution. Both are full-credit papers, not modules.

faqs.question: Is HR Analytics covered as a standalone subject?
faqs.answer: Yes. HR Analytics appears twice in the curriculum. Human Resource Metrics and Analytics in Sem 3 covers dashboards and engagement metrics. HR Analytics in Sem 4 covers predictive models and data-driven HR decision-making. Both are standalone papers.

chooseIf: You want a NAAC A++ public state university degree. No other online MBA at this fee tier matches this combination.
Indian Labour Legislation, Industrial Relations, and Counseling Skills as standalone subjects matter for your compliance-focused or manufacturing HR career.
Government job eligibility and PSU recruitment credibility are priorities. Public university degrees carry inherent weight.
Rs 1,02,000 total (Rs 25,500/semester) fits your budget with zero-cost EMI directly through the university.
consider: National brand recognition matters more than accreditation grade. Amity, NMIMS, and MUJ have stronger pan-India MBA brand positioning.
Vignan at Rs 90,000 is Rs 12,000 cheaper with NAAC A+ and NIRF #70, with HR consulting and OB depth.
Galgotias at Rs 86,400 is the cheapest NAAC A+ option in the NCR region.
If premium LMS, polished delivery, and active placement support are non-negotiable, budget for a higher-fee private university programme.
